title: Oreogrammitis coredrosora
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis coredrosora belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. This family consists of about 2,500 species, with members found in various parts of the world. Oreogrammitis coredrosora is a genus of ferns that is endemic to the island of New Guinea. The species is classified as a terrestrial fern, meaning it grows on the ground rather than in a tree or epiphyte. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Oreogrammitis coredrosora is likely part of the subfamily Grammitidoideae, but further research is needed to confirm its exact classification.
